revision.text:
{{神社 |画像 = |名称 = 倭文神社 |所在地 = [[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]穂坂町宮久保6051　 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 44 | 緯度秒 = 13 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 29 | 経度秒 = 05 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神 = [[天羽槌雄神|天羽槌雄命]]
[[天棚機姫神|天棚機姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |別名 = 降宮 |地図 = Japan Yamanashi }} '''倭文神社'''（しずりじんじゃ）は、[[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]にある神社である。 ===歴史=== [[甲斐国]]の[[延喜式]]内社の20社の1つとして数えられ、[[甲斐国志]]には倭文神社降宮明神と記されている。[[穂坂村|穂坂町]]の総社で宮久保、柳平、三ツ沢三村の[[産土神|産神]]で郷中最も格式の高い神社である。[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011|　倭文神社の詳細｜山梨県神社庁] 境内掲示に、倭文は「しずおり」で、[[麻]]などの[[繊維]]を赤・青などに染めて横糸として織った[[古代]][[織物]]である、と記されている。また、穂坂[[勅旨牧|御牧]]が栄えたころ、御牧の役人の妻や娘などが中心となって織った精巧な織物でこれらの女性たちが[[技芸]]の上達を祈るために天羽槌雄命・天棚機姫命を祀ったのが、この神社の起りである。降宮は「おりみや」で、織宮を意味する。[[江戸時代]]に[[江戸幕府|幕府]]は一二石五斗の[[社領]]を[[寄進]]した、と記されている。 ===祭神=== *[[天羽槌雄神|天羽槌雄命]] *[[天棚機姫神|天棚機姫命]] ==本宮倭文神社== {{神社 |画像 = |名称 = 本宮倭文神社 |所在地 = [[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]穂坂町柳平3195 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 44 | 緯度秒 = 54 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 29 | 経度秒 = 41 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神 = [[天羽槌雄神|天羽槌雄命]]
[[天棚機姫神|天棚機姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |別名 = |地図 = Japan Yamanashi }} '''本宮倭文神社'''は、[[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]にある神社である。 ===歴史=== 宮久保に鎮座する倭文神社の山宮である七夕社と上村組にあった神明社、窪村組の貴船社、[[武田氏|武田]]時代の烽火台といわれる[[城山]]の天神社を、貴船社の社地に合併して、[[1959年]]（[[昭和]]34年）、許可を得て本宮倭文神社と改称した神社である。[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007|　倭文神社の詳細｜山梨県神社庁] ===祭神=== *[[天羽槌雄神|天羽槌雄命]]（七夕社） *[[天棚機姫神|天棚機姫命]]（七夕社） *[[天照大神]]（神明社） *[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大神]]（神明社） *[[イザナミ|伊弉冊命]]（貴船社） *[[菅原道真]]（天神社） ==参考文献・サイト== {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しすりしんしや}} [[Category:山梨県の神社]] [[Category:山梨県の旧郷社]]

[[天棚機姫神|天棚機姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |別名 = |地図 = Japan Yamanashi }} '''本宮倭文神社'''は、[[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]にある神社である。 ===歴史=== 宮久保に鎮座する倭文神社の山宮である七夕社と上村組にあった神明社、窪村組の貴船社、[[武田氏|武田]]時代の烽火台といわれる[[城山]]の天神社を、貴船社の社地に合併して、[[1959年]]（[[昭和]]34年）、許可を得て本宮倭文神社と改称した神社である。[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007|　倭文神社の詳細｜山梨県神社庁] ===祭神=== *[[天羽槌雄神|天羽槌雄命]]（七夕社） *[[天棚機姫神|天棚機姫命]]（七夕社） *[[天照大神]]（神明社） *[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大神]]（神明社） *[[イザナミ|伊弉冊命]]（貴船社） *[[菅原道真]]（天神社） ==参考文献・サイト== {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しすりしんしや}} [[Category:山梨県の神社]] [[Category:山梨県の旧郷社]] [[Category:韮崎市の建築物]] [[Category:韮崎市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori). These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}'''..."
revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "Your Name"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto'''.
== Major Shidori Shrines ==
The following shrines are listed in the '''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}''':
* '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)}} (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}, [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''Shizuka Shrine (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – {{ill|Shizuka Shrine|ja|静神社}} ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|ja|那珂市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)}} (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市}}, [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)}} (Hoshiyama, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|ja|富士宮市}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社}}''' ({{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|ja|三島市}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|鈴鹿市]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)}} (Imada, {{ill|Maizuru, Kyoto|ja|舞鶴市}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)}} (Mikawachi, {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|ja|与謝野町}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)}} (Maruyama, {{ill|Asago, Hyogo|ja|朝来市}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)}} (Shidori, {{ill|Tottori, Tottori|ja|鳥取市}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yurihama)|ja|倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)}} (Miyauchi, {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|ja|湯梨浜町}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)}} (Shizu, {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|ja|倉吉市}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]])
Other notable Shidori Shrines include:
* '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tono, Iwate|ja|遠野市}}, [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)}} (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama, Okayama|ja|津山市}}, [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)}} (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyogo|ja|南あわじ市}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Oomiwa Shrine|ja|大甕神社}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south.
== Related Topics ==
* '''{{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)
[[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[ja:倭文神社]]
[[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:倭文神社]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]]{{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]]

''Shidori Shrine'' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine ''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}'' (also known as ''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto'', ''Ameno Hachio'', or ''Takehatsuchi'') as the ''deity of weaving'', alongside ''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}'' (also known as ''Amano Yachichihime'' or ''Amanoorihime''). These shrines were established by the ''{{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}'', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The ''origin of these shrines is attributed to'' the ''{{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社}}'' in ''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to ''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}'', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of ''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}''. Therefore, these shrines provide ''important historical evidence'' regarding ''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).''
Additionally, in the ''2016 animated film "Your Name"'', the fictional ''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社}}'' is depicted as enshrining ''Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto''.
== Major Shidori Shrines ==
The following shrines are listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'':
* ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)}} (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}, [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * ''Shizuka Shrine (静神社)'' in ''Kuji District, Hitachi Province'' – {{ill|Shizuka Shrine|ja|静神社}} ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|ja|那珂市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Koma District, Kai Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)}} (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市}}, [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Fuji District, Suruga Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)}} (Hoshiyama, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|ja|富士宮市}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Tagata District, Izu Province'' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including ''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社}}'' ({{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|ja|三島市}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Suzuka District, Ise Province'' – ''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}}'' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|鈴鹿市]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Kasa District, Tango Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)}} (Imada, {{ill|Maizuru, Kyoto|ja|舞鶴市}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Yosa District, Tango Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)}} (Mikawachi, {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|ja|与謝野町}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Asago District, Tajima Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)}} (Maruyama, {{ill|Asago, Hyogo|ja|朝来市}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Takakusa District, Inaba Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)}} (Shidori, {{ill|Tottori, Tottori|ja|鳥取市}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Kawamura District, Hōki Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yurihama)|ja|倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)}} (Miyauchi, {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|ja|湯梨浜町}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Kume District, Hōki Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)}} (Shizu, {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|ja|倉吉市}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]])
Other notable Shidori Shrines include:
* ''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)}}'' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tono, Iwate|ja|遠野市}}, [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * ''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)}}'' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)}} (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama, Okayama|ja|津山市}}, [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)}} (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyogo|ja|南あわじ市}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]])
Additionally, ''{{ill|Oomiwa Shrine|ja|大甕神社}}'' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines ''Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto''. Similarly, ''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社}}'' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south.

Additionally, ''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大甕神社]]'' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:日立市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines ''Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto''. Similarly, ''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:石井神社]]'' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:笠間市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south.

Similarly, ''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:石井神社]]'' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:笠間市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==

* ''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:倭文神社 (遠野市)]]'' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tono, Iwate|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:遠野市]], [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * ''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]]'' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara, Nara|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:奈良市]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:倭文神社 (津山市油木北)]] (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama, Okayama|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:津山市]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)]] (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyogo|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:南あわじ市]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]])
Additionally, ''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:大甕神社]]'' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:日立市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines ''Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto''. Similarly, ''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:石井神社]]'' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:笠間市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* ''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]]'' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * ''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]]'' (also known as Amanoorihime)

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 倭文神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=韮崎市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|13.2|N|138|29|6.1|E}}}} |所在地 = [[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]穂坂町宮久保6051 | 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 44 | 緯度秒 = 13.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 29 | 経度秒 = 06.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-19 |祭神 = [[天羽槌雄神|天羽槌雄命]]
[[天棚機姫神|天棚機姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |別名 = 降宮 |地図 = Japan Yamanashi }} '''倭文神社'''（しずりじんじゃ）は、[[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]にある神社である。 ==倭文神社== ===歴史=== [[甲斐国]]の[[延喜式]]内社の20社の1つとして数えられ、[[甲斐国志]]には倭文神社降宮明神と記されている。[[穂坂村|穂坂町]]の総社で宮久保、柳平、三ツ沢三村の[[産土神|産神]]で郷中最も格式の高い神社である。[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011|　倭文神社の詳細｜山梨県神社庁] 境内掲示に、倭文は「しずおり」で、[[麻]]などの[[繊維]]を赤・青などに染めて横糸として織った[[古代]][[織物]]である、と記されている。また、穂坂[[勅旨牧|御牧]]が栄えたころ、御牧の役人の妻や娘などが中心となって織った精巧な織物でこれらの女性たちが[[技芸]]の上達を祈るために天羽槌雄命・天棚機姫命を祀ったのが、この神社の起りである。降宮は「おりみや」で、織宮を意味する。[[江戸時代]]に[[江戸幕府|幕府]]は一二石五斗の[[社領]]を[[寄進]]した、と記されている。 ===祭神=== *[[天羽槌雄神|天羽槌雄命]] *[[天棚機姫神|天棚機姫命]] {{-}} ==本宮倭文神社== {{神社 |名称 = 本宮倭文神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=韮崎市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E}}}} |所在地 = [[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]穂坂町柳平3195 |位置 = {{Coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E|region:JP}} |祭神 = [[天羽槌雄神|天羽槌雄命]]
[[天棚機姫神|天棚機姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |別名 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|lat_deg=35|lat_min=44|lat_sec=54|lon_deg=138|lon_min=29|lon_sec=41|label={{Jbr|本宮|倭文|神社}}|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} }} '''本宮倭文神社'''は、[[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]にある神社である。 ===歴史=== 宮久保に鎮座する倭文神社の山宮である七夕社と上村組にあった神明社、窪村組の貴船社、[[武田氏|武田]]時代の烽火台といわれる[[城山]]の天神社を、貴船社の社地に合併して、[[1959年]]（[[昭和]]34年）、許可を得て本宮倭文神社と改称した神社である。[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007|　倭文神社の詳細｜山梨県神社庁] ===祭神=== *[[天羽槌雄神|天羽槌雄命]]（七夕社） *[[天棚機姫神|天棚機姫命]]（七夕社） *[[天照大神]]（神明社） *[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大神]]（神明社） *[[イザナミ|伊弉冊命]]（貴船社） *[[菅原道真]]（天神社） {{-}} ==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しすりしんしや}} [[Category:山梨県の神社]] [[Category:山梨県の旧郷社]] [[Category:韮崎市の建築物]] [[Category:韮崎市の歴史]] [[Category:菅原道真]] == See Also == * ''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|12=draft}} '' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * ''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}} '' (also known as Amanoorihime) {{citation needed|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|12=draft}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation needed|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)}} }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]] [[Category:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)]] [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] [[ja:韮崎市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:富士宮市]] [[ja:鍬戸神社]] [[ja:倉吉市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (遠野市)]] [[ja:遠野市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:奈良市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (津山市油木北)]] [[ja:津山市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)]] [[ja:南あわじ市]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[ja:日立市]] [[ja:石井神社]] [[ja:笠間市]] {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|de|---|ja|郷社|zh|---}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = {{ill|October 10|de|---|ja|10月10日|zh|---}} |Alternate_name = Orimiya (降宮) |Map = Japan Yamanashi }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|---|ja|神社|zh|---}} located in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}. ==History== The shrine is counted among the twenty {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} of {{ill|Kai Province|de|Provinz Kai|ja|甲斐国|zh|甲斐国}}, as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|de|---|ja|延喜式|zh|---}}. In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|de|---|ja|甲斐国志|zh|---}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|de|---|ja|穂坂村|zh|---|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ubusuna-no-kami|de|---|ja|産土神|zh|---|lt=Guardian deity|lt_ja=産神}}) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|de|---|ja|勅旨牧|zh|---|lt=Imperial Pasture}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the {{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代|zh|江户时代}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|de|Tokugawa-Shogunat|ja|江戸幕府|zh|江户幕府}} reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.
===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}} {{-}} ==Honmiya Shizuri Jinja (本宮倭文神社)== {{Shrine |Name = '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) |Image = {{Image needed|cat={{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E}}}} |Address = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}} Hosaka-chō Yanagidaira 3195 |Position = {{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} (small)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|de|---|ja|村社|zh|---|lt=Village shrine}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = {{ill|October 10|de|---|ja|10月10日|zh|---}} |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|---|ja|神社|zh|---}} in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on {{ill|Jōyama|de|---|ja|城山|zh|---}} (attributed to the {{ill|Takeda clan|de|Klan Takeda|ja|武田氏|zh|武田氏}} era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|de|---|ja|天照大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|de|---|ja|豊受大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Izanami|de|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|zh|伊邪那美}} (Kifune-sha) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|de|---|ja|菅原道真|zh|---}} (Tenjin-sha) {{-}} ==References== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Shinto shrines It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|de|---|ja|勅旨牧|zh|---|lt=Imperial Pasture}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the {{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代|zh|江户时代}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|de|Tokugawa-Shogunat|ja|江戸幕府|zh|江户幕府}} reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.
===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}} {{-}} ==Honmiya Shizuri Jinja (本宮倭文神社)== {{Shrine |Name = '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) |Image = {{Image needed|cat={{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E}}}} |Address = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}} Hosaka-chō Yanagidaira 3195 |Position = {{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} (small)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|de|---|ja|村社|zh|---|lt=Village shrine}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = {{ill|October 10|de|---|ja|10月10日|zh|---}} |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|---|ja|神社|zh|---}} in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on {{ill|Jōyama|de|---|ja|城山|zh|---}} (attributed to the {{ill|Takeda clan|de|Klan Takeda|ja|武田氏|zh|武田氏}} era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|de|---|ja|天照大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|de|---|ja|豊受大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Izanami|de|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|zh|伊邪那美}} (Kifune-sha) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|de|---|ja|菅原道真|zh|---}} (Tenjin-sha) {{-}} ==References== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Shinto shrines It is described as the highest-ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|de|---|ja|勅旨牧|zh|---|lt=Imperial Pasture}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the {{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代|zh|江户时代}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|de|Tokugawa-Shogunat|ja|江戸幕府|zh|江户幕府}} reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.
===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}} {{-}} ==Honmiya Shizuri Jinja (本宮倭文神社)== {{Shrine |Name = '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat={{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E}}}} |Address = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}} Hosaka-chō Yanagidaira 3195 |Position = {{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} (small)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|de|---|ja|村社|zh|---|lt=Village shrine}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = {{ill|October 10|de|---|ja|10月10日|zh|---}} |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|---|ja|神社|zh|---}} in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on {{ill|Jōyama|de|---|ja|城山|zh|---}} (attributed to the {{ill|Takeda clan|de|Klan Takeda|ja|武田氏|zh|武田氏}} era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|de|---|ja|天照大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|de|---|ja|豊受大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Izanami|de|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|zh|伊邪那美}} (Kifune-sha) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|de|---|ja|菅原道真|zh|---}} (Tenjin-sha) {{-}} ==References== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Shinto shrines {{Shrine |Name = '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat={{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|13.2|N|138|29|6.1|E}}}} |Address = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}} Hosaka-chō Miyakubo 6051 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 44 |lat_sec = 13.2 |N(S) = N |lon_deg = 138 |lon_min = 29 |lon_sec = 06.1 |E(W) = E |map_code = JP-19 |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|de|---|ja|郷社|zh|---}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = {{ill|October 10|de|---|ja|10月10日|zh|---}} |Alternate_name = Orimiya (降宮) |Map = Japan Yamanashi }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|---|ja|神社|zh|---}} located in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}. ==History== The shrine is counted among the twenty {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} of {{ill|Kai Province|de|Provinz Kai|ja|甲斐国|zh|甲斐国}}, as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|de|---|ja|延喜式|zh|---}}. In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|de|---|ja|甲斐国志|zh|---}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|de|---|ja|穂坂村|zh|---|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ubusuna-no-kami|de|---|ja|産土神|zh|---|lt=Guardian deity|lt_ja=産神}}) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|de|---|ja|勅旨牧|zh|---|lt=Imperial Pasture}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the {{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代|zh|江户时代}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|de|Tokugawa-Shogunat|ja|江戸幕府|zh|江户幕府}} reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.
===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}} {{-}} ==Honmiya Shizuri Jinja (本宮倭文神社)== {{Shrine |Name = '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat={{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E}}}} |Address = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}} Hosaka-chō Yanagidaira 3195 |Position = {{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}}

Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} Former {{ill|Gōsha|de|---|ja|郷社|zh|---}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = {{ill|October 10|de|---|ja|10月10日|zh|---}} |Alternate_name = Orimiya (降宮) |Map = Japan Yamanashi }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|---|ja|神社|zh|---}} located in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}. ==History== The shrine is counted among the twenty {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} of {{ill|Kai Province|de|Provinz Kai|ja|甲斐国|zh|甲斐国}}, as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|de|---|ja|延喜式|zh|---}}. In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|de|---|ja|甲斐国志|zh|---}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|de|---|ja|穂坂村|zh|---|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ubusuna-no-kami|de|---|ja|産土神|zh|---|lt=Guardian deity|lt_ja=産神}}) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|de|---|ja|勅旨牧|zh|---|lt=Imperial Pasture}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the {{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代|zh|江户时代}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|de|Tokugawa-Shogunat|ja|江戸幕府|zh|江户幕府}} reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.
===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}} {{-}} ==Honmiya Shizuri Jinja (本宮倭文神社)== {{Shrine |Name = '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat={{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E}}}} |Address = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}} Hosaka-chō Yanagidaira 3195 |Position = {{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} (small)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|de|---|ja|村社|zh|---|lt=Village shrine}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = {{ill|October 10|de|---|ja|10月10日|zh|---}} |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|---|ja|神社|zh|---}} in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on {{ill|Jōyama|de|---|ja|城山|zh|---}} (attributed to the {{ill|Takeda clan|de|Klan Takeda|ja|武田氏|zh|武田氏}} era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|de|---|ja|天照大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|de|---|ja|豊受大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Izanami|de|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|zh|伊邪那美}} (Kifune-sha) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|de|---|ja|菅原道真|zh|---}} (Tenjin-sha) {{-}} revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat={{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|13.2|N|138|29|6.1|E}}}} |Address = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}} Hosaka-chō Miyakubo 6051 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 44 |lat_sec = 13.2 |N(S) = N |lon_deg = 138 |lon_min = 29 |lon_sec = 06.1 |E(W) = E |map_code = JP-19 |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|12=draft}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|de|---|ja|郷社|zh|---}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = {{ill|October 10|de|---|ja|10月10日|zh|---}} |Alternate_name = Orimiya (降宮) |Map = Japan Yamanashi }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|---|ja|神社|zh|---}} located in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}. ==History== The shrine is counted among the twenty {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} of {{ill|Kai Province|de|Provinz Kai|ja|甲斐国|zh|甲斐国}}, as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|de|---|ja|延喜式|zh|---}}. In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|de|---|ja|甲斐国志|zh|---}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|de|---|ja|穂坂村|zh|---|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ubusuna-no-kami|de|---|ja|産土神|zh|---|lt=Guardian deity|lt_ja=産神}}) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|de|---|ja|勅旨牧|zh|---|lt=Imperial Pasture}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|12=draft}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the {{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代|zh|江户时代}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|de|Tokugawa-Shogunat|ja|江戸幕府|zh|江户幕府}} reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.
===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|12=draft}} {{-}} ==Honmiya Shizuri Jinja (本宮倭文神社)== {{Shrine |Name = '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat={{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E}}}} |Address = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}} Hosaka-chō Yanagidaira 3195 |Position = {{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|12=draft}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} (small)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|de|---|ja|村社|zh|---|lt=Village shrine}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = {{ill|October 10|de|---|ja|10月10日|zh|---}} |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|---|ja|神社|zh|---}} in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on {{ill|Jōyama|de|---|ja|城山|zh|---}} (attributed to the {{ill|Takeda clan|de|Klan Takeda|ja|武田氏|zh|武田氏}} era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|12=draft}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|de|---|ja|天照大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|de|---|ja|豊受大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Izanami|de|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|zh|伊邪那美}} (Kifune-sha) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|de|---|ja|菅原道真|zh|---}} (Tenjin-sha) {{-}} In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|de|---|ja|甲斐国志|zh|---}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|de|---|ja|穂坂村|zh|---|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ubusuna-no-kami|de|---|ja|産土神|zh|---|lt=Guardian deity|lt_ja=産神}}) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|de|---|ja|勅旨牧|zh|---|lt=Imperial Pasture}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|12=draft}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the {{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代|zh|江户时代}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|de|Tokugawa-Shogunat|ja|江戸幕府|zh|江户幕府}} reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.
===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|12=draft}} {{-}} ==Honmiya Shizuri Jinja (本宮倭文神社)== {{Shrine |Name = '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat={{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E}}}} |Address = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}} Hosaka-chō Yanagidaira 3195 |Position = {{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|12=draft}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} (small)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|de|---|ja|村社|zh|---|lt=Village shrine}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = {{ill|October 10|de|---|ja|10月10日|zh|---}} |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|---|ja|神社|zh|---}} in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on {{ill|Jōyama|de|---|ja|城山|zh|---}} (attributed to the {{ill|Takeda clan|de|Klan Takeda|ja|武田氏|zh|武田氏}} era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|12=draft}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|de|---|ja|天照大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|de|---|ja|豊受大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Izanami|de|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|zh|伊邪那美}} (Kifune-sha) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|de|---|ja|菅原道真|zh|---}} (Tenjin-sha) {{-}} Former {{ill|Gōsha|de|---|ja|郷社|zh|---}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = {{ill|October 10|de|---|ja|10月10日|zh|---}} |Alternate_name = Orimiya (降宮) |Map = Japan Yamanashi }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|---|ja|神社|zh|---}} located in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}. ==History== The shrine is counted among the twenty {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} of {{ill|Kai Province|de|Provinz Kai|ja|甲斐国|zh|甲斐国}}, as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|de|---|ja|延喜式|zh|---}}. In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|de|---|ja|甲斐国志|zh|---}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|de|---|ja|穂坂村|zh|---|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ubusuna-no-kami|de|---|ja|産土神|zh|---|lt=Guardian deity|lt_ja=産神}}) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|de|---|ja|勅旨牧|zh|---|lt=Imperial Pasture}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the {{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代|zh|江户时代}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|de|Tokugawa-Shogunat|ja|江戸幕府|zh|江户幕府}} reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.
===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} (small)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|de|---|ja|村社|zh|---|lt=Village shrine}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = {{ill|October 10|de|---|ja|10月10日|zh|---}} |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|---|ja|神社|zh|---}} in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on {{ill|Jōyama|de|---|ja|城山|zh|---}} (attributed to the {{ill|Takeda clan|de|Klan Takeda|ja|武田氏|zh|武田氏}} era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|de|---|ja|天照大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|de|---|ja|豊受大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Izanami|de|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|zh|伊邪那美}} (Kifune-sha) * {{Shrine |Name = '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat={{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|13.2|N|138|29|6.1|E}}}} |Address = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}} Hosaka-chō Miyakubo 6051 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 44 |lat_sec = 13.2 |N(S) = N |lon_deg = 138 |lon_min = 29 |lon_sec = 06.1 |E(W) = E |map_code = JP-19 |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|de|---|ja|郷社|zh|---}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = {{ill|October 10|de|---|ja|10月10日|zh|---}} |Alternate_name = Orimiya (降宮) |Map = Japan Yamanashi }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|---|ja|神社|zh|---}} located in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}. ==History== The shrine is counted among the twenty {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} of {{ill|Kai Province|de|Provinz Kai|ja|甲斐国|zh|甲斐国}}, as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|de|---|ja|延喜式|zh|---}}. In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|de|---|ja|甲斐国志|zh|---}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|de|---|ja|穂坂村|zh|---|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ubusuna-no-kami|de|---|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|zh|---|lt=Guardian deity|lt_ja=産神}}) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|de|---|ja|勅旨牧|zh|---|lt=Imperial Pasture}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the {{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代|zh|江户时代}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|de|Tokugawa-Shogunat|ja|江戸幕府|zh|江户幕府}} reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.
===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} (small)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|de|---|ja|村社|zh|---|lt=Village shrine}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = {{ill|October 10|de|---|ja|10月10日|zh|---}} |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|---|ja|神社|zh|---}} in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on {{ill|Jōyama|de|---|ja|城山|zh|---}} (attributed to the {{ill|Takeda clan|de|Klan Takeda|ja|武田氏|zh|武田氏}} era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|de|---|ja|天照大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|de|---|ja|豊受大神|zh|---}} (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Izanami|de|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|zh|伊邪那美}} (Kifune-sha) * {{Shrine |Name = '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat={{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|13.2|N|138|29|6.1|E}}}} |Address = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}} Hosaka-chō Miyakubo 6051 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 44 |lat_sec = 13.2 |N(S) = N |lon_deg = 138 |lon_min = 29 |lon_sec = 06.1 |E(W) = E |map_code = JP-19 |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|de|---|ja|郷社|zh|---}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = {{ill|October 10|de|---|ja|10月10日|zh|---}} |Alternate_name = Orimiya (降宮) |Map = Japan Yamanashi }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|---|ja|神社|zh|---}} located in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|de|Präfektur Yamanashi|ja|山梨県|zh|山梨县}}{{ill|Nirasaki City|de|Stadt Nirasaki|ja|韮崎市|zh|韮崎市}}. ==History== The shrine is counted among the twenty {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|---|ja|式内社|zh|---}} of {{ill|Kai Province|de|Provinz Kai|ja|甲斐国|zh|甲斐国}}, as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|de|---|ja|延喜式|zh|---}}. In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|de|---|ja|甲斐国志|zh|---}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|de|---|ja|穂坂村|zh|---|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ubusuna-no-kami|de|---|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|zh|---|lt=Guardian deity|lt_ja=産神}}) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|de|---|ja|勅旨牧|zh|---|lt=Imperial Pasture}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the {{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代|zh|江户时代}}, the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|de|Tokugawa-Shogunat|ja|江戸幕府|zh|江户幕府}} reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.
===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|de|---|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|---|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命}} * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no
