Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Village shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] (attributed to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]] era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q111674494]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat=[[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|13.2|N|138|29|6.1|E}}}} |Address = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]] Hosaka-chō Miyakubo 6051 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 44 |lat_sec = 13.2 |N(S) = N |lon_deg = 138 |lon_min = 29 |lon_sec = 06.1 |E(W) = E |map_code = JP-19 |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = Orimiya (降宮) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |prefecture= {{ill|Nirasaki|qid=Q290326}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.737|N|138.4850277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ==History== The shrine is counted among the twenty {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]], as listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=|1=Kaikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577871}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|ja|穂坂村|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosaka Town|qq=|1=Hosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597051}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ubusunagami|Guardian deity]]) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-Ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|lt=Imperial Pasture|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi-maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[:en:Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa shogunate]] reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.

Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Village shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] (attributed to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]] era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Tanabata-sha) * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] (Shinmei-sha) * [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q111674494]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat=[[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|13.2|N|138|29|6.1|E}}}} |Address = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]] Hosaka-chō Miyakubo 6051 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 44 |lat_sec = 13.2 |N(S) = N |lon_deg = 138 |lon_min = 29 |lon_sec = 06.1 |E(W) = E |map_code = JP-19 |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = Orimiya (降宮) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |prefecture= {{ill|Nirasaki|qid=Q290326}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.737|N|138.4850277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ==History== The shrine is counted among the twenty {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]], as listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=|1=Kaikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577871}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|ja|穂坂村|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosaka Town|qq=|1=Hosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597051}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ubusunagami|Guardian deity]]) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-Ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|lt=Imperial Pasture|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi-maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[:en:Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa shogunate]] reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.

Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} {{-}} ==Honmiya Shizuri Jinja (本宮倭文神社)== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Honmiya Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]] Hosaka-chō Yanagidaira 3195 |Position = {{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Village shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] (attributed to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]] era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q111674494]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat=[[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|13.2|N|138|29|6.1|E}}}} |Address = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]] Hosaka-chō Miyakubo 6051 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 44 |lat_sec = 13.2 |N(S) = N |lon_deg = 138 |lon_min = 29 |lon_sec = 06.1 |E(W) = E |map_code = JP-19 |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = Orimiya (降宮) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |prefecture= {{ill|Nirasaki|WD=Q290326}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.737|N|138.4850277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ==History== The shrine is counted among the twenty {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]], as listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=|1=Kaikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577871}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|ja|穂坂村|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosaka Town|qq=|1=Hosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597051}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ubusunagami|Guardian deity]]) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-Ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|lt=Imperial Pasture|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi-maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[:en:Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa shogunate]] reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.
===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Village shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] (attributed to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]] era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q111674494]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat=[[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|13.2|N|138|29|6.1|E}}}} |Address = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]] Hosaka-chō Miyakubo 6051 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 44 |lat_sec = 13.2 |N(S) = N |lon_deg = 138 |lon_min = 29 |lon_sec = 06.1 |E(W) = E |map_code = JP-19 |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = Orimiya (降宮) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |prefecture= {{ill|Nirasaki|WD=Q290326}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.737|N|138.4850277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ==History== The shrine is counted among the twenty {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]], as listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=|1=Kaikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577871}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|ja|穂坂村|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosaka Town|qq=|1=Hosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597051}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ubusunagami|Guardian deity]]) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-Ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|lt=Imperial Pasture|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi-maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[:en:Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa shogunate]] reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.
===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Village shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] (attributed to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]] era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = Orimiya (降宮) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |prefecture= {{ill|Nirasaki|WD=Q290326}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.737|N|138.4850277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ==History== The shrine is counted among the twenty {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]], as listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|1=Kaikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577871}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|ja|穂坂村|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosaka Town|1=Hosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597051}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ubusunagami|Guardian deity]]) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-Ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|lt=Imperial Pasture|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi-maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[:en:Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa shogunate]] reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.

Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Village shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] (attributed to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]] era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = Orimiya (降宮) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |prefecture= {{ill|Nirasaki|WD=Q290326}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.737|N|138.4850277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ==History== The shrine is counted among the twenty {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]], as listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|1=Kaikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577871}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|ja|穂坂村|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosaka Town|1=Hosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597051}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ubusunagami|Guardian deity]]) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-Ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|lt=Imperial Pasture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi-maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[:en:Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa shogunate]] reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.

Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] (attributed to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]] era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q111674494]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat=[[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|13.2|N|138|29|6.1|E}}}} |Address = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]] Hosaka-chō Miyakubo 6051 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 44 |lat_sec = 13.2 |N(S) = N |lon_deg = 138 |lon_min = 29 |lon_sec = 06.1 |E(W) = E |map_code = JP-19 |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = Orimiya (降宮) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |prefecture= {{ill|Nirasaki|WD=Q290326}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.737|N|138.4850277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ==History== The shrine is counted among the twenty {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]], as listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|1=Kaikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577871}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|ja|穂坂村|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosaka Town|1=Hosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597051}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ubusunagami|Guardian deity]]) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-Ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|lt=Imperial Pasture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi-maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[:en:Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa shogunate]] reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.
===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] (attributed to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]] era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Tanabata-sha) * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] (Shinmei-sha) * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} (Shinmei-sha) * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] (Kifune-sha) * [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Tenjin-sha) {{-}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} ==See Also== * ''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}'' (also called Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * (also known as Amanoorihime) }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|version=101989837|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q111674494]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat=[[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|13.2|N|138|29|6.1|E}}}} |Address = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]] Hosaka-chō Miyakubo 6051 |lat_deg = 35 |lat_min = 44 |lat_sec = 13.2 |N(S) = N |lon_deg = 138 |lon_min = 29 |lon_sec = 06.1 |E(W) = E |map_code = JP-19 |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = Orimiya (降宮) |Map = Japan Yamanashi |prefecture= {{ill|Nirasaki|WD=Q290326}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.737|N|138.4850277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shizuri Jinja''' (倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ==History== The shrine is counted among the twenty {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]], as listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. In the {{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|1=Kaikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577871}}, it is recorded as “Shizuri Jinja Orimiya Myōjin.” {{ill|Hosaka Town|ja|穂坂村|lt=Hosaka-chō|lt_ja=穂坂町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosaka Town|1=Hosaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597051}} regarded it as its sōja, and it serves as the tutelary deity ([[:en:Ubusunagami|Guardian deity]]) of Miyakubo, Yanagidaira, and Mitsusawa. It is described as the highest-Ranking shrine in that region.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] On a signboard in the shrine precincts, it is noted that “Shizuri” (倭文) is pronounced “shizuori,” referring to an ancient textile woven with threads of hemp or other fibers dyed in red and blue. It is said that, when the imperial ranch ({{ill|Chokushi-maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|lt=Imperial Pasture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi-maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}) in Hosaka flourished, wives and daughters of the ranch officials wove fine textiles and prayed for the improvement of their craft by enshrining {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. “Orimiya” (降宮) is pronounced “orimiya” and literally means “weaving shrine.” During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[:en:Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa shogunate]] reportedly dedicated 12 koku and 5 to (units of land rice yield) as shrine territory.
===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} {{-}} ==Honmiya Shizuri Jinja (本宮倭文神社)== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) |image = {{Image needed|cat=[[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]}} Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]] Hosaka-chō Yanagidaira 3195 |Position = {{coord|35|44|54|N|138|29|41|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined = {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=---|lt_ja=天棚機姫命|lt=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|1=Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Tanabatahime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |Sacred_object = |Shakaku = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Annual_Festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Alternate_name = |Map = {{Location map |Japan Yamanashi |lat_deg=35 |lat_min=44 |lat_sec=54 |lon_deg=138 |lon_min=29 |lon_sec=41 |label={{Jbr|Honmiya|Shizuri|Jinja}} |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center |mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg |marksize=20 |caption= }} }} '''Honmiya Shizuri Jinja''' (本宮倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki City]]. ===History=== This shrine is said to be the Yamamiya (mountain shrine) known as the Tanabata-sha of the Shizuri Jinja that stands in Miyakubo. It also consolidated the Shinmei-sha of the Kamimura-gumi district, the Kifune-sha of the Kubomura-gumi district, and the Tenjin-sha on [[:en:Shiroyama|Shiroyama]] (attributed to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]] era as a beacon tower). In 1959 (Shōwa 34), with official permission, these were merged at the site of the Kifune-sha and renamed Honmiya Shizuri Jinja.[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007　Shizuri Jinja details | Yamanashi Prefectural Shrine Agency] ===Enshrined Deities=== * {{ill|Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=---|lt_ja=|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=天羽槌雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hahitsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (Tanabata-sha) * {{ill|Ame no Tanabatahime no
